enonce.texte: Match the environmental vocabulary words with their correct definitions. This will help you understand key terms related to waste management and environmental issues.

questions.texte: Match each word to its definition.

sousQuestions.texte: Rubbish disposal
sousQuestions.reponse: The process of getting rid of waste materials.

sousQuestions.texte: Waste
sousQuestions.reponse: Unwanted or unusable materials, substances, or by-products.

sousQuestions.texte: Toxic
sousQuestions.reponse: Poisonous or harmful to living organisms.

sousQuestions.texte: Recycle
sousQuestions.reponse: To convert waste into reusable material.

sousQuestions.texte: Contaminate
sousQuestions.reponse: To make something impure by exposure to or addition of a poisonous or polluting substance.

enonce.texte:
Read the following text about waste management problems in urban areas and answer the questions below.

"In many African cities, waste management has become a serious problem. Rapid urbanization and population growth have led to an increase in the amount of waste produced. Inadequate disposal systems have resulted in the contamination of water sources and the spread of diseases. Many people burn their rubbish, which releases toxic gases into the air. This situation is dangerous for both human health and the environment. However, some communities have started recycling initiatives. They collect plastic bottles and paper to sell to recycling companies. These efforts help reduce pollution and create jobs for local people."

questions.texte: Answer the following comprehension questions about the text.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify two causes of waste management problems mentioned in the text.
sousQuestions.reponse: Two causes are rapid urbanization and population growth.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain the consequences of inadequate waste disposal systems.
sousQuestions.reponse: Inadequate disposal systems have resulted in the contamination of water sources and the spread of diseases.

sousQuestions.texte: Describe the benefits of recycling initiatives mentioned in the text.
sousQuestions.reponse: Recycling initiatives help reduce pollution and create jobs for local people.

enonce.texte: The causative structure 'have + object + past participle' is used to talk about services we arrange for someone else to do. For example: 'We should have our garbage collected regularly.'

questions.texte: Complete the sentences using the causative 'have' structure.

sousQuestions.texte: We need to ______ our rubbish (collect) ______ more frequently.
sousQuestions.reponse: We need to have our rubbish collected more frequently.

sousQuestions.texte: They ______ their old furniture (recycle) ______ by a local company.
sousQuestions.reponse: They had their old furniture recycled by a local company.

sousQuestions.texte: We should ______ our water (test) ______ for contamination.
sousQuestions.reponse: We should have our water tested for contamination.

intitule: Causes and Effects of Waste Problems

enonce.texte:
Read the text below about waste management challenges and discuss the causes and effects.

"In many cities, the lack of proper waste collection services has led to mountains of garbage on streets. This waste attracts rats and insects, which spread diseases. Burning waste releases harmful chemicals that cause respiratory problems. The government has tried to solve this by providing more bins and trucks, but the problem persists due to lack of funding and public awareness."

questions.texte: Answer the questions about the causes and effects of waste problems.

sousQuestions.texte: List two causes of the waste problem mentioned in the text.
sousQuestions.reponse: Two causes are: 1) lack of proper waste collection services, and 2) lack of funding and public awareness.

sousQuestions.texte: List two effects of the waste problem mentioned in the text.
sousQuestions.reponse: Two effects are: 1) the spread of diseases by rats and insects, and 2) respiratory problems from burning waste.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain why the government's solution has not been completely successful.
sousQuestions.reponse: The government's solution has not been completely successful because of lack of funding and lack of public awareness.

enonce.texte: Practice using the causative 'have' structure in environmental contexts. Remember: have + object + past participle.

questions.texte: Rewrite the sentences using the causative structure.

sousQuestions.texte: Someone collects our garbage every Tuesday. (have)
sousQuestions.reponse: We have our garbage collected every Tuesday.

sousQuestions.texte: A company will recycle our plastic bottles. (have)
sousQuestions.reponse: We will have our plastic bottles recycled by a company.

sousQuestions.texte: They planted trees in the community. (have)
sousQuestions.reponse: They had trees planted in the community.

enonce.texte: You are a community leader. Write a proposal to address waste management problems in your neighborhood.

questions.texte: Write a short proposal with practical solutions.

sousQuestions.texte: State the problem clearly.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'Our community is facing serious waste management problems. Garbage is piling up on the streets.'

sousQuestions.texte: Propose 3 solutions using the causative 'have' structure.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: '1. We should have our garbage collected at least twice a week. 2. We should have recycling bins placed in every neighborhood. 3. We should have regular clean-up campaigns organized by the local council.'

sousQuestions.texte: Explain the benefits of your proposed solutions.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'These solutions will reduce pollution, prevent diseases, and create a cleaner and healthier environment for everyone.'

enonce.texte:
Read the text and analyze the relationship between causes and effects in waste management.

"The increase in plastic waste has become a major environmental challenge. Because plastic does not decompose easily, it accumulates in landfills and oceans. This causes harm to marine life and pollutes the soil. As a result, governments are implementing bans on single-use plastics. Recycling programs are being established to reduce the amount of plastic waste."

questions.texte: Analyze the causes and effects described in the text.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify the main cause of the environmental challenge described.
sousQuestions.reponse: The main cause is the increase in plastic waste.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify two effects of plastic accumulation.
sousQuestions.reponse: Two effects are: 1) harm to marine life, and 2) pollution of the soil.

sousQuestions.texte: Analyze the solutions proposed to address the problem.
sousQuestions.reponse: The solutions proposed are: 1) bans on single-use plastics, and 2) the establishment of recycling programs.

enonce.texte: You are in charge of an environmental awareness campaign in your community. Design a campaign to address waste management issues.

questions.texte: Create a comprehensive environmental awareness campaign.

sousQuestions.texte: Give your campaign a name and a slogan.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: Name: 'Clean City, Healthy Life'. Slogan: 'Reduce, Reuse, Recycle for a Better Tomorrow!'

sousQuestions.texte: Describe the main activities of your campaign.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example activities: 1. Weekly clean-up campaigns, 2. Door-to-door education on waste sorting, 3. Distribution of recycling bins, 4. Workshops on composting organic waste, 5. Collaboration with local recycling companies.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain how you will measure the success of your campaign.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'Success will be measured by: 1) Reduction in waste on streets, 2) Increase in recycling rates, 3) Number of households participating in clean-up campaigns, 4) Surveys to measure public awareness about waste management.'

enonce.texte: The causative 'have' is used to talk about arranging for someone else to do something. Practice using it in various contexts.

questions.texte: Choose the correct option to complete the sentences.

sousQuestions.texte: We need to ______ our water ______ for contamination.
sousQuestions.reponse: have / tested

sousQuestions.texte: The city ______ the waste ______ to a landfill.
sousQuestions.reponse: has / transported

sousQuestions.texte: We should ______ our rubbish ______ every week.
sousQuestions.reponse: have / collected

enonce.texte: Write a short report on waste management problems in your area and propose solutions.

questions.texte: Write a report with the following sections.

sousQuestions.texte: Introduction: State the problem.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'This report addresses the waste management problems in our community and proposes practical solutions.'

sousQuestions.texte: Causes: Explain why the problem exists.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'The main causes of the problem are: 1) Inadequate waste collection services, 2) Lack of public awareness, 3) Limited recycling facilities.'

sousQuestions.texte: Effects: Describe the consequences.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'The effects include: 1) Pollution of streets and water sources, 2) Health problems for residents, 3) Negative impact on the environment.'

sousQuestions.texte: Recommendations: Propose solutions using causative structures.
sousQuestions.reponse: Example: 'We recommend: 1) Having our waste collected more frequently, 2) Having recycling bins installed in every neighborhood, 3) Having educational programs organized for the community.'
